sources.title: Acephalous Societies: Bands and Tribes

dependencyEdges.note: The scoped process separates curds from animal milk, so managed dairy animals are required to supply its raw material.

sources.title: Earliest evidence for cheese making in the sixth millennium BC in northern Europe
sources.source_locator: Abstract: frames cheese production as processing animal milk and reports abundant milk fat in specialized strainer vessels used to separate curds from whey.

dependencyEdges.note: The evidence-backed technique uses purpose-made perforated pottery vessels to separate fat-rich curds from whey.

sources.title: Earliest evidence for cheese making in the sixth millennium BC in northern Europe

sources.title: Earliest evidence for cheese making in the sixth millennium BC in northern Europe

name: Catalhoyuk Bast-Fiber Weaving

sources.title: Earliest Evidence for Textile Technologies

dependencyEdges.note: Earlier string, basketry, twining, and wrapping techniques form the documented technical lineage from which preserved woven cloth developed.

sources.title: Earliest Evidence for Textile Technologies

sources.title: 30,000-year-old wild flax fibers

name: Plow

Draft animals greatly expanded plow use, but hand-pulled or pushed forms mean animal husbandry is not strictly required.
